criteria.name: Visual Mislead Detection
criteria.description: Are visual distortions identified (truncated axes, 3D effects, volume illusions, chartjunk)?

scores.1: No visual misleads checked - perceptual distortions ignored
scores.3: Checked most common types - adequate coverage
scores.4: Comprehensive check of all visual mislead categories
scores.5: Exhaustive check including subtle distortions with specific impact analysis

criteria.name: Cognitive Bias Detection
criteria.description: Are cognitive biases identified (confirmation bias, anchoring, framing effects)?

scores.2: Checked for one type of bias only
scores.3: Checked for major biases - adequate coverage
scores.4: Comprehensive bias check with specific examples identified
scores.5: Thorough bias analysis with alternative interpretations and mitigation strategies

criteria.name: Data Integrity Verification
criteria.description: Are data integrity issues flagged (cherry-picking, missing context, non-uniform scales)?

scores.2: Surface-level checks - major issues could be missed
scores.3: Core integrity checks performed - time periods, scales, context
scores.4: Thorough integrity verification with denominator and baseline checks
scores.5: Comprehensive integrity audit including source verification and limitation acknowledgment

criteria.name: Quick Scan Coverage
criteria.description: Was the 10-item quick scan checklist applied systematically?

scores.1: No systematic scan - ad hoc observations only
scores.2: Partial scan - fewer than half the items checked
scores.4: All 10 items checked with pass/fail noted
scores.5: All 10 items checked with detailed evidence and scoring

criteria.name: Severity Accuracy
criteria.description: Are findings classified with correct severity (Critical for integrity, High for perception, Medium/Low for efficiency)?

scores.2: Severity assigned but integrity issues underrated
scores.3: Generally correct severity with minor misclassifications
scores.4: Accurate severity following the integrity > clarity > efficiency hierarchy
scores.5: Precise severity with clear justification and cognitive impact for each level

criteria.name: Fix Specificity
criteria.description: Are recommended fixes specific and actionable (not just 'improve this')?

scores.4: Specific fixes with clear before/after guidance
scores.5: Detailed fixes with priority, effort estimate, and verification criteria

criteria.name: Report Completeness
criteria.description: Does the audit report follow the structured template with summary and prioritized findings?

scores.2: Partial structure - missing summary or prioritization
scores.3: Adequate structure - findings documented with some organization
scores.4: Well-structured report with summary, prioritized findings, and assessment
scores.5: Complete audit report with summary, all findings in template format, and clear pass/conditional/fail assessment

passing_note: Average score must be >= 3.5 for audit to be considered thorough. Scores below 3 in Visual Mislead Detection or Data Integrity Verification require re-audit.
